exemplar.title: What is the relationship between the viscosity of a fluid being drained through a pipe of variable radius and the final velocity of the liquid exiting the pipe?

detailedExemplarData.title: What is the relationship between the viscosity of a fluid being drained through a pipe of variable radius and the final velocity of the liquid exiting the pipe?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question clearly and precisely stated in the title block.
Research question is restated clearly in the introduction, reinforcing focus.
Variables table clearly identifies and explains independent, dependent, and controlled variables.
Methodology outlines each step in detail, making the procedure highly reproducible.
weaknesses: Number of trials for each liquidâradius combination is not specified, leaving reproducibility incomplete.
Minor formatting and typographical mistakes (e.g., broken tables) that occasionally impede clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student communicates the topic and research question accurately and maintains strong focus throughout (band 5â6). The methodology is mostly complete and appropriate (band 3â4), but omissions such as unspecified trial numbers prevent full-band 6 performance. Overall, a score of 5 reflects high quality with only minor lapses in completeness and clarity.

strengths: Opening narrative effectively contextualizes the problem with a real-world example.
Definitions and equations for Bernoulliâs Law are clear and use correct terminology and units.
Derivation of Torricelliâs Law and Poiseuilleâs Law are accurate and well explained.
Propagation of uncertainty calculation is presented clearly with correct formulae.
weaknesses: Typographical errors in key terms (e.g., âViscositvâ) undermine precision.
Continuity equation is miswritten (Aâvâ = Aâvâ instead of Aâvâ = Aâvâ).
Sporadic formatting glitches reduce sustained professional precision.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates good subject knowledge and appropriate use of terminology (band 3â4), with clear application of core fluid-mechanics concepts. However, occasional errors in equations and typos prevent the consistently flawless precision required for the top band, warranting a score of 4.

strengths: Data analysis is clear and relevant, with propagated uncertainties and comparison to theoretical models.
Discussion interprets how pipe radius correlates with velocity, demonstrating insightful analysis.
Argument follows a logical sequence, consistently linking evidence to claims.
Extensions section offers valuable future directions, showing creative intellectual engagement.
Critical evaluation highlights unrealistic model predictions and reflects on limitations.
weaknesses: Limited depth in statistical analysis (e.g., no RÂ² values) and alternative interpretations.
Evaluative comments are brief and not integrated throughout the entire analysis.
Some argumentative passages (e.g., entrance-length corrections) lack full justification.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student delivers good research analysis (band 4â6) and a well-developed argument (band 7â9), consistently focused on the research question. Critical evaluation is present but not pervasive (band 4â6). On balance, the overall performance aligns with the lower end of band 7â9, justifying a score of 7.

strengths: Title page includes word count and full table of contents, facilitating navigation.
Bibliography is extensive and includes relevant sources, supporting credibility.
Figures and tables are captioned and referenced, aiding comprehension.
weaknesses: Table of contents and several tables suffer rendering glitches, hindering readability.
Some figures lack labelled axes and units, reducing visual clarity.
Controlled variables table is split across pages without proper headers.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report adheres to EE structural conventions with clear sectioning and citations (band 3â4), but formatting artifacts and inconsistent visual presentation prevent truly polished delivery. A score of 3 reflects effective overall presentation with room for improvement in clarity and formatting.

strandSelections.reasoning: Technical language such as âdynamic viscosity,â âentrance length,â and âTorricelliâs Lawâ is accurate and generally consistent; symbols are defined and units given.  Sporadic typographical errors ("Viscositv", mis-placed subscripts) and occasional formatting glitches prevent the sustained precision characteristic of the 5-6 band, so a top-of-band 3-4 score is appropriate.

The essay follows a logical sequence (background â variables â experiment â theory â data â conclusion) and consistently links evidence to claims.  The conclusion synthesises theoretical and empirical findings, and the evaluation section reflects on limitations.  Some argumentative passages (e.g., entrance-length correction assumptions) lack full justification, so coherence is strong but not exemplary across the whole essay; a score at the lower end of the 7-9 band (7) is justified.

The methodology includes a labelled diagram, full materials list, identification of independent, dependent and controlled variables, uncertainty treatment and a repeatable step-by-step procedure.  Nevertheless, key omissions remain: viscosity is taken from secondary sources rather than measured, number of trials is not specified, and the data-collection strategy does not address potential turbulent flow.  Therefore the strand meets most descriptors of the 3-4 band but lacks the comprehensive, consistently appropriate selection expected in 5-6.

Core fluid-mechanics concepts (Bernoulli, Poiseuille, continuity, Reynolds number) are applied correctly in the main, and the student recognises limits of each model.  Calculations link theory to experiment, and cited densities/viscosities are appropriate.  However, mis-handling of Poiseuille for short pipes and reliance on assumed Reynolds numbers indicate partial rather than fully coherent understanding, placing the work solidly in the 3-4 band with the higher score.

Critical evaluation appears mainly in one dedicated section, identifying sources of error (uncertain viscosity, limited radii range) and suggesting extensions.  Nonetheless, evaluative comments are brief and not consistently integrated throughout the analysis.  The work thus meets descriptors for âgood evaluation with some critical analysisâ (band 4-6) but lacks the depth and pervasiveness expected for the higher bands; a mid-band score of 4 is selected.

The student clearly states the subject area (Physics), the general topic (Fluid Mechanics) and an explicit research question in both the title block and the introduction.  Background paragraphs link the everyday observation of soda dispensers to the more formal statement of purpose, demonstrating clear focus.  Minor lapses in formatting (e.g., broken tables, a few typographical mistakes) do not impede overall comprehension.  Hence the work merits the upper band where topic is communicated accurately and effectively, but a perfectâband score is limited by the occasional clarity issues.

Figures, tables and graphs are included, captioned and referenced in the text, aiding comprehension.  Axes are labelled and uncertainties plotted.  However, several tables are improperly rendered and two figures are missing scale clarity, so the visual layout, while effective overall, is not flawless.  A solid but not perfect score within the top band is therefore appropriate.

The research question â ârelationship between viscosity, pipe radius and final velocityâ â is precisely stated, appears on the title page and in the introduction, and is revisited in the conclusion.  Subsequent experimental design and theoretical modelling sections remain tightly linked to that question, showing a strong connection to the ensuing discussion.  Some breadth (two independent variables rather than one) prevents complete laser-like focus, so the award sits at 5 rather than 6.

Data are processed into flow rates and velocities with propagated uncertainties; trends are graphed and compared with two theoretical models, and reasons for divergence are discussed.  While analysis is clear and relevant, it does not probe deeply into statistical significance, model refinement, or alternative interpretations.  This constitutes the upper level of âgoodâ analysis (band 4-6) rather than the sustained, comprehensive insight required for 7-9.

The essay contains a title page with word count, full table of contents, logical section numbering, bibliography and in-text citations.  Despite some formatting artefacts (HTML tags, broken tables), the overall structure adheres to EE conventions and allows the reader to follow the work easily.  These minor inconsistencies reduce professional polish but still satisfy the highest presentation band.

annotations.comment: The research question is restated clearly in the introduction, reinforcing focus and coherence.
The research question is clearly and precisely stated in the title block, providing strong focus for the investigation.
Consider italicizing or clearly formatting the research question to distinguish it from surrounding text and enhance readability.
The table of contents contains rendering glitchesârows and columns overlapâmaking navigation harder.
Despite some artifacts, the table of contents follows a logical structure, facilitating easy navigation of sections.
The opening narrative effectively contextualizes the problem with a realâworld example, demonstrating clear subject understanding.
The word count is correctly stated and within the 4 000âword limit, meeting formatting requirements.
The definitions and equations for Bernoulliâs Law are clear, using correct terminology and proper unit specification.
In the Poiseuille section, include a brief note on the limitations of laminar flow assumptions under high Reynolds numbers.
The variables table clearly identifies and explains independent, dependent, and controlled variables.
The controlled variables table is split across pages and lacks a proper header row, hindering readability.
Using only three viscosities limits exploration; consider adding more intermediate values or measuring viscosity experimentally.
The methodology section outlines each step in detail, making the procedure highly reproducible.
The number of trials for each liquidâradius combination is not specified, leaving reproducibility incomplete.
Specify the number of repeats per trial and the randomization strategy to strengthen methodological rigor.
Consider stating a brief hypothesis or expected trend to clarify the investigationâs direction before proceeding.
The section header has a typo (âViscositvâ instead of âViscosityâ), which undermines precision in terminology.
The continuity equation is miswritten as Q = Aâ vâ = Aâ vâ; it should be Q = Aâ vâ = Aâ vâ.
The safety discussion is thorough, clearly identifying risks and protective measures for acetone and glassware.
The environmental considerations section demonstrates thoughtful handling and disposal procedures.
The derivation of Torricelliâs Law is correct and well explained, showing solid command of fluid principles.
When simplifying Bernoulliâs Law, include an intermediate algebraic step to justify neglecting vâ explicitly.
The Poiseuille derivation is accurate and demonstrates clear understanding of viscosityâs role.
Add more detail on estimating entrance length (Lâ) to support use of the modified HagenâPoiseuille model.
The discussion of an unrealistic 199 m/s Poiseuille prediction shows good critical evaluation of model assumptions.
Consider referencing typical gravityâdriven flow velocities to quantify how unrealistic the calculated value is.
Table 3 is neatly formatted and clearly presents the theoretical velocities for each condition.
Enhance the caption by specifying what each column and unit represents to make the table selfâcontained.
Figure 2 effectively visualizes predicted data but lacks labeled axes and units, reducing clarity.
The propagation of uncertainty calculation is presented clearly with correct formulae.
Include a worked numerical example for one radiusâliquid combination to illustrate uncertainty propagation.
The discussion clearly interprets how pipe radius correlates with velocity, demonstrating insightful analysis.
Consider reporting a quantitative measure (e.g., RÂ²) to support claims about the strength of the radiusâvelocity relationship.
A stray page number (â19â) appears in the conclusion, indicating a leftover formatting artifact.
The extensions section offers valuable future directions, demonstrating creative intellectual engagement.
The bibliography is extensive and includes relevant sources, supporting the investigationâs credibility.
Some bibliography entries lack DOIs or consistent URL formatting; apply a standard citation style uniformly.
